lint.command: bash ci/lint-knowledgebase.sh && bash ci/check-architecture.sh && bash ci/check-enterprise-policy.sh && bash ci/check-orchestrator-layer.sh

nonBlockingIssues.description: FLOW-INVENTORY.md Coverage Notes still says 'packet pending' and 'will be created in later milestones' but all 11 packets now exist
INDEX.md Flows section says 'Five flow packets (flows 1–5) are now available; the remaining six will be added in later milestones' but all 11 now exist
Duplicate section numbering — two '## 10.' headings (Related Documentation and Open Decisions). Open Decisions should be '## 11.'
Duplicate section numbering — two '## 10.' headings (Related Documentation and Open Decisions). Open Decisions should be '## 11.'
Access-control boundaries in Event/Listener section duplicate some points from Security Considerations section — consider consolidating to avoid drift
Non-Canonical path entry 'Legacy intake endpoints' has status 'Partially disabled' but no explicit replacement or no-replacement note

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: flow packet section numbering must be sequential 1–11. When inserting a new section, all subsequent sections must be renumbered. This prevents the duplicate section number issue seen in hr-payroll.md and reporting-export.md.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Two flow packets (hr-payroll.md, reporting-export.md) have duplicate '## 10.' headings after Event/Listener sections were added without renumbering subsequent sections.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: when all flow packets for a milestone are complete, workers must update INDEX.md and FLOW-INVENTORY.md prose to remove 'packet pending' and 'coming in later milestones' language.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: INDEX.md and FLOW-INVENTORY.md still contain stale prose claiming only 5 of 11 packets exist when all 11 are complete.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify threshold for when Event/Listener Boundaries section is required vs optional in flow packets. Currently 4 of 11 packets lack this section (auth-identity, tenant-admin-management, catalog-setup-readiness, invoice-dealer-finance) with no explicit guidance on when to include it.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: docs-flows-library-canonical-seams feature scoped the section to 'where they materially affect a major flow' but no written convention defines the materiality threshold.
